reasoning: John Morgan has repeatedly flirted with the idea of a 2026 run for Florida Governor, and recently discussed launching a new third party. However, at a May 14, 2025 Capital Tiger Bay luncheon, he explicitly stated that he intends to wait until September 2026 (after the major party primaries) before deciding whether to enter the race. While Florida law requires him to formally register with his new third party by June 12, 2025, to run on its ticket (365 days before the June 12, 2026, qualifying deadline), changing his voter affiliation or forming a party does not constitute formally announcing his own candidacy. Since he clearly and recently stated his timeline is over a year from now, an announcement before July 1, 2025 is highly improbable unless he makes an abrupt structural change to his strategy or accidentally declares while filing party paperwork.
